mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is ideal for production deployments requiring efficient orchestration of large language models. Key trade-offs include the need for specific hardware accelerators and potential complexity in setup.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d command-line interface, which is necessary for managing the deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf llm-d-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv llm-d /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the latest CLI version.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Create a GKE cluster with 3 nodes.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Fetch credentials for the cluster.

steps.title: Verify Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in the cluster are ready and operational.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster and their statuses.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up autoscaling and monitoring for the llm-d deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Deploy the autoscaler configuration.
> Deploy monitoring tools.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes the llm-d deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

kubectl delete deployment llm-d-deployment

gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Delete the llm-d deployment.
> Delete the GKE cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your data. This step will upgrade llm-d to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get all -n llm-d --export -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/llm-d-v0.5.1.yaml

kubectl rollout status deployment/llm-d-deployment

```

> Backup current llm-d resources.
> Apply the new version of llm-d.
> Check the status of the deployment to ensure it is updated.
commands: kubectl get all -n llm-d --export -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is inaccessible.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and tag in your deployment configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application is failing to start due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and adjust resource requests/limits.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough CPU resources available for the deployment.

**Fix:**
Scale up the cluster or reduce resource requests in the deployment.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient permissions for the service account used by the deployment.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service account has the necessary roles and permissions.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new hardware accelerators and improved inference scheduling algorithms.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.5.0, review the new configuration options for inference scheduling.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n llm-d' showing all pods in the 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
